history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is lava (L), so moving forward is deadly. I need to reach the goal (G) located at (4,5). The safe adjacent cell to my east (right) is a floor (.). I will turn right to face east, which allows me to move toward the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) at row 6, column 4 is a floor (.). The cells to my left (west, row 7, col 3) and right (east, row 7, col 5) are lava and wall, respectively, so turning would not help. Moving forward is the only safe and productive option. The goal G is located at row 5, column 2, but the direct path west from row 5, column 4 is blocked by lava. I need to explore further to find a way around the lava; advancing north will reveal more of the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell ahead (5,3) is safe floor. Directly west is also safe, but the path to the goal at (5,1) is blocked by lava at (5,2). Moving forward will reveal more of the map to the north and potentially uncover a route around the walls and lava. Turning would not immediately help reach the goal, so advancing is the best exploratory action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is two cells to the left, but blocked by lava at (6,2). The cell ahead (north) is a wall, and the cell to the right (east) is also a wall. The only unexplored direction is behind (south), which may offer a route around the wall. I will turn clockwise to begin facing south.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (indicated by `^`). Its forward cell is a wall `#`, so it cannot move forward. The cell to the right (east) is floor `.`, while the left is also a wall. Turn right to face east, so it can then move forward into the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is floor, safe to step on. The cell to the right (east) is lava, and to the left is a wall. The goal is two cells to the right and one row same, but lava blocks direct east path. Moving forward will take a step north, from where we can later turn and head east to reach the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The forward cell (north) is floor (`.`), safe to move. The right cell (east) is also floor, but moving forward will explore further north where the corridor seems to open up. The goal is not yet visible, so exploring north is a reasonable step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
My forward cell is a wall (#), the right cell is lava (L), and the left cell is a wall (#). The only safe direction is behind me, where I came from. I need to turn around to head back and explore other areas. I will start by turning left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The forward cell is a wall (#), the right cell is a wall (#), and the left cell is a floor (.). The only safe visible path is to turn left and then move forward onto the floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a floor (`.`), so I can safely move forward. Turning left would face a lava cell (`L`), and turning right would face a wall (`#`). The goal is visible ahead and to the left, but lava blocks the direct path. Advancing forward will allow me to see more of the map and find a safe route around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center, facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a floor (`.`), which is safe to move into. The left cell is also floor, and the right cell is a wall (`#`). The goal `G` is visible to the northwest, but a lava cell `L` blocks the western path from the current row. Moving forward onto the floor will allow the agent to explore more of the map and find a safe route to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
